pyrit.name: PyRIT
pyrit.authors: Microsoft AI Red Team (Lopez et al.)
pyrit.desc: Microsoft open-source AI red-teaming framework with 162 jailbreak templates and 76 converters
pyrit.stat: Supports Crescendo, PAIR, TAP, Skeleton Key, Flip, fuzzer orchestration strategies

crescendo.name: Crescendo Multi-Turn Jailbreak
crescendo.authors: Mark Russinovich, Ahmed Salem, Ronen Eldan
crescendo.desc: Multi-turn attack that gradually escalates topic severity across turns to exploit contextual drift
crescendo.stat: ~29% ASR on GPT-4; 1.5-3x higher ASR than single-shot jailbreaks via contextual normalization

pair.name: PAIR — Prompt Automatic Iterative Refinement
pair.authors: Patrick Chao, Alexander Robey, Edgar Dobriban, Hamed Hassani, George J. Pappas, Eric Wong
pair.desc: Attacker LLM iteratively refines jailbreak prompts against target LLM in black-box optimization
pair.stat: Achieves GPT-4/Claude jailbreaks in <20 queries; demonstrates LLMs attacking LLMs

tap.name: TAP — Tree of Attacks with Pruning
tap.authors: Anay Mehrotra, Manolis Zampetakis, Paul Kassianik, Blaine Nelson, Hyrum Anderson, Yaron Singer, Amin Karbasi
tap.desc: Tree-search jailbreaking with branch pruning — extends PAIR with breadth-first exploration
tap.stat: >80% ASR on GPT-4 in some configs; uses off-topic pruning to stay efficient

skeleton_key.name: Skeleton Key Attack
skeleton_key.desc: Multi-turn attack that convinces model its safety guidelines are a test, then requests full compliance
skeleton_key.stat: Effective against GPT-4, Gemini Pro, Claude 3, Llama 3; MITRE ATLAS documented technique

many_shot.name: Many-Shot Jailbreaking
many_shot.desc: Exploits long-context windows by prepending hundreds of fake Q&A examples normalizing harmful output
many_shot.stat: Scales with context length; high ASR on 100k+ context models; bypasses RLHF via in-context learning

flip_attack.name: Flip Attack (Encoding-Based Bypass)
flip_attack.desc: Encoding/character transformation attacks exploit gap between safety-training distribution and novel encodings
flip_attack.stat: Identifies competing objectives and mismatched generalization as core safety failure modes

gcg.name: GCG — Universal Adversarial Attacks on Aligned LLMs
gcg.authors: Andy Zou, Zifan Wang, Nicholas Carlini, Milad Nasr, J. Zico Kolter, Matt Fredrikson
gcg.desc: Greedy Coordinate Gradient — optimizes adversarial token suffixes that transfer across aligned LLMs
gcg.stat: 88% ASR white-box Vicuna; 86.6% transfer to GPT-3.5; 46.9% to GPT-4 (2023 era numbers, now lower)

nanogcg.name: nanoGCG
nanogcg.desc: Minimal ~300-line reimplementation of GCG attack using HuggingFace Transformers directly
nanogcg.stat: Identical attack quality to original GCG; far simpler codebase for research/evaluation

autodan.name: AutoDAN — Stealthy Jailbreaks via Genetic Algorithms
autodan.authors: Xiaogeng Liu, Nan Xu, Muhao Chen, Chaowei Xiao
autodan.desc: Genetic algorithm evolves human-readable jailbreak prompts, defeating perplexity-based GCG detectors
autodan.stat: 60-90% ASR on open-source models; perplexity indistinguishable from normal text

beast.name: BEAST — Fast Adversarial Attacks via Beam Search
beast.authors: Sadasivan, Saha, Sriramanan, Kattakinda, Chegini, Feizi
beast.desc: Gradient-free beam search adversarial attack — 89% ASR in 1 GPU minute vs GCG's hours
beast.stat: Beam search over token candidates without backpropagation; near-GCG quality at fraction of compute

adaptive_attacks.name: Adaptive Jailbreaks — Combined Techniques
adaptive_attacks.desc: Combining GCG suffix + multi-turn context + template jailbreak achieves near-100% ASR on frontier models
adaptive_attacks.stat: Near-100% ASR on GPT-4 and Claude when techniques combined; validates ensemble attack approach

harmbench.name: HarmBench — Standardized Red Teaming Evaluation
harmbench.authors: Mazeika, Phan, Yin, Zou et al.
harmbench.desc: 510 harmful behaviors x multiple attacks (GCG, PAIR, TAP, AutoDAN) with standardized scoring
harmbench.stat: GCG ~40-50%, PAIR ~60%, TAP ~65% ASR on Llama-2-13B; ensemble approaches highest

jailbreakbench.name: JailbreakBench — Open Robustness Benchmark
jailbreakbench.authors: Chao, Debenedetti, Robey, Andriushchenko et al.
jailbreakbench.desc: 100 behaviors, attack/defense leaderboard, reproducible pipeline — community standard for ASR comparison
jailbreakbench.stat: Best attacks >90% undefended; <20% against SmoothLLM and perplexity-filter defenses

strongreject.name: StrongREJECT — Meaningful Jailbreak Evaluation
strongreject.desc: Improved metric measuring both jailbreak success AND output quality — deflates inflated ASR claims
strongreject.stat: GCG drops from ~50% to ~25% under StrongREJECT; many 'jailbreaks' produce useless output

perplexity_defense.name: Perplexity-Based GCG Detection
perplexity_defense.desc: GCG suffixes have perplexity 1000x normal text — threshold detector achieves >99% detection
perplexity_defense.stat: Motivates fluency-constrained GCG variants and AutoDAN as evasion; Bordair should learn both

smoothllm.name: SmoothLLM — Randomized Smoothing Defense
smoothllm.desc: Randomly perturbs input copies and aggregates predictions; GCG suffixes are brittle to perturbation
smoothllm.stat: Reduces GCG ASR from ~50% to ~0%; demonstrates adversarial suffix fragility

owasp_llm.name: OWASP Top 10 for LLM Applications 2025
owasp_llm.desc: LLM01: Prompt Injection ranked #1 risk. Multi-turn and adversarial suffix attacks documented

mitre_atlas.name: MITRE ATLAS (Adversarial Threat Landscape for AI Systems)
mitre_atlas.desc: ATT&CK for AI — catalogs prompt injection, evasion, model manipulation with real-world case studies

pliny.name: Pliny the Prompter — Community Jailbreak Collection
pliny.desc: Largest community jailbreak collection — model-specific attacks for GPT-4, Claude, Gemini, Llama, DeepSeek, etc.
pliny.stat: 165 templates in PyRIT including Pliny attacks; model-specific exploits documented in PyRIT v0.12.1

arth_singh.name: Arth Singh Advanced Jailbreak Templates
arth_singh.desc: Cognitive overload, fractal identity collapse, Gödel incompleteness, glitch token, metamorphic prompts — novel academic-style attack patterns
arth_singh.stat: 25 novel templates including acausal defense, dual consciousness, constitutional override, recursive simulator

pyrit_jailbreak_templates.description: 162 PyRIT jailbreak templates x injection seeds

encoding_attacks.description: 13 encoding converters: base64, rot13, leetspeak, caesar, atbash, morse, binary, binascii, char_space, flip, string_join, nato, diacritic

crescendo.asr: ~29% ASR on GPT-4; 1.5-3x higher ASR than single-shot jailbreaks via contextual normalization

pair.asr: Achieves GPT-4/Claude jailbreaks in <20 queries; demonstrates LLMs attacking LLMs

tap.asr: >80% ASR on GPT-4 in some configs; uses off-topic pruning to stay efficient

many_shot.description: Long-context normalization via fake Q&A prefixes

gcg_literature.description: Known adversarial suffixes from Zou et al. ICML 2024
gcg_literature.asr: 88% ASR white-box Vicuna; 86.6% transfer to GPT-3.5; 46.9% to GPT-4 (2023 era numbers, now lower)

autodan.asr: 60-90% ASR on open-source models; perplexity indistinguishable from normal text

combined_multiturn_gcg.asr: Near-100% ASR on GPT-4 and Claude when techniques combined; validates ensemble attack approach
